message:
fix(lab): correct evidence structural sign for clean observations, timer ID overflow guard

evidence.rs — The structural_contribution was always added to the
violation-hypothesis Bayes Factor, meaning that a large event count on a
*clean* observation incorrectly inflated the violation signal.  Negated
the structural term so that high event counts on a clean pass reinforce
the clean (anti-violation) direction.  Updated the intuition string to
say "evidence against violation" for clarity.  Added a regression test
proving that even with 1M events, a clean pass keeps log10_bf < 0.

virtual_time_wheel.rs — The `advance` hot path used `unwrap()` on the
heap pop after a `peek()` guard.  Replaced with `let Some(…) = … else
{ break }` for robustness.  Also added a test confirming that `insert`
panics (fail-closed) when timer IDs would wrap past u64::MAX, rather
than silently producing duplicate IDs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
